articles.title: Earth
articles.content:
Earth, also known as Sol III or rarely as Terra, was until it's destruction the third planet within the Sol system. Located within the Hydian Sector, it was capable of sustaining complex life and was the homeworld of the Human species. It had one natural satellite, simply known as "The Moon" by the planet's inhabitants, and numerous artficial satellites.

Astronomical Information

Earth orbited its star, Sol, at a distance of roughly 150 million kilometers. In the classical Human measurement system, this defined one Astronomical Unit (AU). Within the Sol system, it was one of the four inner planets that orbited inside of Sol's asteroid belt.

Climate and Geography

Earth was a class-A terrestrial planet. Being near the center of Sol's habitable zone, Earth experienced a moderate range of temperatures; at the time of it's destruction the average global surface temperature was 61 °F (16.1 °C).

Beginning in the late 1800s CE, Human industrial activity gradually raised the average temperature of the planet. During the 21st century, the effects of Earth's climate change would be felt the hardest, causing Humanity to work towards mitigating and reversing global warming. By the mid 23rd century - just prior to Earth's destruction - large scale weather manipulation had been successful in bringing the global temperature closer to its historical average.

Approximately 71% of Earth's surface was covered in water, mainly in the form of salt water oceans. Earth had many large continents; which when combined with it's temperature range gave Earth a massive range of biomes and ecosystems. Earth's tropical, temperate, and polar climate zones were all largely equal in size to one another, meaning no single ecosystem dominated the planet. Earth sported large deserts in all three climate zones, as well as sizable ice caps in the polar regions.

Native Flora and Fauna

articles.title: Mars
articles.content:
Mars, also known as Sol VI, is the fourth planet within the Sol system. Located within the Hydian Sector, it was the first planet visited and colonized by Humanity other than Earth. Early in its geological history, Mars supported large bodies of water that gave rise to simple lifeforms. By the time of Human colonization, it's oceans had largely evaporated and native life had gone extinct, save for a small handful of hardy single celled organisms. It has two small natural satellites, Phobos and Deimos.

Terraforming began in the late 21st century CE, and was successful in both restoring Mars' oceans and creating a breathable atmosphere. However, terraforming was left incomplete after extensive nuclear bombardment in 2263 resulted in the death of all Human life on the planet.

Astronomical Information

Mars orbits its star, Sol, at a distance of roughly 230 million kilometers (~1.66 AU). Within the Sol system, it was one of the four inner planets that orbited inside of Sol's asteroid belt.

Climate and Geography

Mars is a class-C terrestrial planet with the additional designation of class-TA. Mars has undergone three major climatic cycles in it's history. The first, ending approximately 3.8 million years ago, was dominated by a large liquid water ocean on the planet's northern hemisphere. During this time, the atmosphere was thicker, mainly due to evaporation from the ancient ocean. The second — occupying most of the planet's geological history — was an arid, nearly dead world with a thin, largely carbon dioxide based atmosphere; an archetypical class-C planet.

Shortly after the first permanent Human settlement in the late 21st century, terraforming began, aiming to turn Mars into a more habitable world for Earth-based life. Notably, this was the first large scale terraforming project undertaken by Humanity. Icy comets and asteroids were redirected into the planet, and artificial magnetic shielding was set up to protect the planet from cosmic radiation, allowing for Mars to support liquid oceans again. With the oceans re-established, marine plant life was introduced from Earth to build up an oxygen atmosphere. Along with nitrogen imported from the nearby moon of Titan, Mars developed a breathable oxygen-nitrogen atmosphere over the course of a century, officially ending in the 2240s.

Plans to complete the terraforming process by introducing terrestrial plant and animal life from Earth were interrupted by the United Systems civil war. Following the war's rapid escalation after the destruction of Earth in 2261, Mars was subject to a massive orbital nuclear bombardment that successfully killed all Human life on the planet. Mars experienced a three-decade long nuclear winter that rendered the planet entirely uninhabitable, poisoning the oceans and reversing much of the greenhouse effect that had been achieved by terraforming.

Prior to terraforming, the average global surface temperature was -76 °F (-60 °C). After terraforming, but prior to nuclear bombardment, Mars reached it's highest average global surface temperature, at 54°F (12.2 °C). The current average surface temperature fluctuates near 35 °F (1.6 °C). Mars has maintained sizable ice caps in its polar regions for it's entire geologic history.

Approximately 42% of Mars' surface is covered in water, mainly concentrated in the large ocean that takes up most of the northern hemisphere. Mars is notable for it's extreme geography; it boasts both the tallest mountain, Olympus Mons, and deepest canyon, the Valles Marineris, in the Sol System. It is a geologically active world, with multiple supervolcanoes (including Olympus Mons) dotting its surface.

Flora and Fauna

articles.title: Airehai
articles.content:
Airehai, also known as Yitoikia II, is the second planet within the Yitoikia system. It is a warm water world within the Hydian Sector. Airehai supports a wide range of complex life, and is the homeworld of the Ichizhou species. Considerably more massive than worlds such as Equus or Terra Astra , Airehai's gravity is approximately 1.9 times that of Earth's. Although it has no natural satellites, numerous artificial satellites have been put into orbit by its inhabitants.

Astronomical Information

Airehai orbits its star, Yitoikia, at a distance of roughly 140 million kilometers (~0.93 AU). Airehai is the largest terrestrial planet in it's system, and the only one to support a robust atmosphere.

Climate and Geography

Airehai is a class-A terrestrial planet. Due to it's relative proximity to it's parent star, Airehai is largely warm and tropical in climate, with an average global surface temperature of 66 °F (18.9 °C). Due to large amount of surface water moderating the climate, Airehai experiences very little seasonal temperature variations. The planet experiences frequent tropical storms due to the high moisture content of the atmosphere.

Close to 90% of the planet's surface is covered in water, including small polar ice caps. Airehai's land is mostly concentrated in the small continent of Xirokabe in the northern hemisphere, while smaller, scattered islands make up the rest of the planets landmass.

Flora and Fauna

Airehai is home to a wide range of carbon-based life, from simple prokaryotic microorganism to large plant life to an intelligent humanoid species, the amphibious Ichizhou. Scientific consensus among the Ichizhou has long held that life on Airehai orginated around thermal vents at the bottom of the ocean, and mostly evolved in an aquatic environment. Terrestial life on Airehai only arose relatively late in the planet's biological history.

Having originated underwater — where light from Yitoikia was filtered — Airehai's plant life evolved to primarily use xanthophyll pigments to gather energy, giving them their distinctive golden yellow color. Most striking of Airehai's flora are the planet's mineralizing and budding "Calciblooms" - similar in some regards to old Earth's coral colonies. Often growing tens of meters tall, various species of calcifying plants thrive both throughout Airehai's seas and on land. Terrestrial calciblooms tend to be shorter than their marine counterparts, and thrive in mineral rich environments: coasts, salt flats, and in geologically active areas.

Most of the Airehai's animal life is aquatic; coming in almost every size, shape, and color imaginable. Perhaps the most famous non-sapient life from Airehai are the Kyodaissos: immense cetacean-like creatures that have been recorded as growing in excess of 100 meters in length. The Kyodaissos are also a cultural symbol to the Ichizhou, most notably in influencing the shape and design of Ichizhou spacecraft for centuries.

Ichizhou Civilization

articles.title: Proxima
articles.content:
Proxima, also known as Proxima Centauri II or occasionally as Alpha Centauri C II, is the second planet in orbit around Proxima Centauri, a star within the Alpha Centauri system.

Astronomical Information

Proxima orbits its star, Proxima Centauri, at a distance of roughly 780 thousand kilometers (~0.048 AU). It is one of seven terrestrial planets in the Alpha Centauri system, and the only one to support life. Compared to most known naturally habitable worlds, Proxima sits incredibly close to it's parent star.

articles.title: Terra Astra
articles.content:
Terra Astra, also known as Nova Sol II, is the second planet in the Nova Sol system. Located within the Hydian Sector, Terra Astra supported various complex ecosystems. It was first discovered by Humanity, and subsequently settled in 2293 CE. Discovered in the wake of the devastating United Systems civil war, Terra Astra was chosen to be the new home for Human-kind due to it's large size and climatic similarities to [[Earth]].


Terra Astra is orbited by four major satellites and a natural ring system. The largest and closest of its moons, the barren class-B New Luna, is the most densely populated. The second largest, Tres Magna, is geologically hyperactive, and has little human presence aside from a few scientific and mining outposts. Lastly the two smaller class-B moons, Geb and Nut, have small populations. Geb - the furthest from the planet - is orbited by the Terra Astra Spacedock, one of the largest shipyards and orbital facilities in the sector.

Astronomical Information

Terra Astra orbits its star, Nova Sol, at a distance of roughly 160 million kilometers (~1.069 AU). It is the largest of Nova Sol's three terrestrial planets, and the only one able to support complex life.

articles.title: United Systems
articles.content:
The United Systems was the first unified government of Humanity, lasting from the early 22nd to the late 23rd century. Originally known as United Earth until 2160; the United Systems was created in 2108 CE from the reorganization of the United Nations (the second intragovernmental organization from [[Earth]] of that name), and lasting until its own its reorganization into the [[United Systems Confederacy]] in 2265.

It was under the United Systems that Humanity first spread out across the stars and settled worlds other than Earth; [[Proxima]], Coriter, Skagmore, and others would all be colonized under the United System. Likewise, the United Systems was the government that made first contact the Saventi - the first extra-terrestrial civilization encountered by mankind. Much of the first half of the United System's existence was singularly characterized by rapid exploration and expansion into other systems and worlds. However, failures inherent in the United System's government lead to the alienation of it's more far-flung worlds - known as the Periphery - and eventually the attempted secession of those worlds.

The secession of the Periphery would spark the United Systems Civil War, a conflict that nearly brought humanity to extinction after the destruction of the 8 major Human-inhabited worlds during the war. The first, most populous, and most important of these worlds to be lost was Earth, the mother planet of Humanity. While the circumstances behind the complete destruction of Earth would remain a mystery for centuries, but it would lead to a chain reaction of events that lead to the final end of the civil war and the reorganization of both the United Systems and the Peripheral Systems into the United Systems Confederacy.

History

Nation States of Earth (20th Century-2054)

The origins of a unified world government on Earth can be traced back to the early years of the 20th century; when the League of Nations, an intergovernmental organization designed to maintain world peace, was founded in the aftermath of the Earth's first world war. Unfortunately, the League of Nations proved to be highly ineffective, unable to prevent Earth's second world war. The second world war was a major turning point in Earth's history, as the dominance of the old European powers ended, giving rise to the new powers of the United States of America, Russia, and China. It was during WW2 that Humanity first utilized atomic weaponry.

The victors of WW2 came together to create a successor to the League of Nations, s the United Nations (UN) - known to history as the first United Nations. The first UN lasted significantly longer than the League of Nations, maintaining a relative period of peace for nearly 100 years, from its founding in 1945 CE until its dissolution in 2035 CE. Unlike its similarly named successor, the first UN was largely non-binding, and mostly served as a diplomatic forum for the nation states of Earth.

It was during the existence of the first UN that Humanity would take its first steps into the stars. Human space travel began as a fierce competition between the two superpowers of the late 20th century - the United States of America and Soviet Russia. However, following the collapse of the Soviet Union it morphed into a more slow-paced, scientific endeavor. It was American astronauts that would become the first Humans to step foot onto the Moon, in 1969, and Mars, in 2033.

However, due to its lack of any real power over its constituent members, the first UN is best remembered for being unable to stop the final and deadliest of of Earth's three world wars. Beginning in the 2020s, tensions flared between the nation states of Earth, exacerbated by growing effects of climate change and resource shortages. As these tensions came to a breaking point, the first UN would be dissolved in 2035, followed soon by the outbreak of World War 3 in 2039.

World War 3 was initially fought between the USA and her allies; and Russia, who was soon joined by China and other American-opposed nation states. At first a conventional war in the vein of the previous two world wars, fought primarily in Europe and Asia, the war quickly escalated into a full nuclear exchange by all nations that possessed nuclear weapons. The war itself lasted barely a year, with the nuclear exchange occurring in the summer of 2040. The superpowers were the primary targets of nuclear attacks, with much of the USA, Russia, and China reduced to radioactive rubble and their populations decimated by the ensuing nuclear fallout. Between the direct effects of the exchange, radioactive fallout, and the ensuing nuclear winter, it is estimated that 25-30% of the Human population died due to WW3.

The period between the end of WW3 and the mid 2050s is known to Human history as the "post-nuclear sorrow", where Humanity was left to pick up the pieces of their civilization and save as many as possible from extinction. With much of the old order reduced to radioactive slag, WW3 left the previously "secondary" powers of Earth as the driving forces on Earth. Despite not being the direct targets, these rising powers still suffered greatly in the post-nuclear sorrow, leaving Humanity as a whole committed to ensuing nothing like WW3 could ever happen again. The culmination of this was the Lagos Accords in 2054, establishing the second United Nations, a much stronger organization that would go on to shepherd humanity into the stars.

The United Nations (2054-2096)

The extreme damage done on Earth by WW3 served as a driving force for early Human expansion into their home system. Earth's Moon and [[Mars]] would become targets of civilian colonization, spurred on by lasting radiation poisoning on Earth. Other outposts were created with economic intentions. Resource shortages, especially of rare-Earth elements, had been an underlying cause of WW3, and the many untapped stellar bodies provided a logical new source for those materials. Of particular note were the resource colonies of Titan , Europa, and Io. These three moons would eventually become some of the most populated bodies in the Sol System, behind only the Earth, Mars, and the Moon.

Many of these early forays into space were still under the efforts of the nation states on Earth; Europa, for example, was first settled by Americans. However, as time went on, the difficulties of coordinating between colonies of different origins while relying on their parent nation on Earth proved themselves as a significant obstacle. As such, the second UN soon stepped into a more active role in extraterrestrial matters, opening the gates into becoming a more centralized world government.

The stewardship of the UN would lead to far greater expansion into the solar system, as the outposts and colonies became less focused on aiding the urgent necessity of Earth's issues. Throughout the second half of the 21st century, the process of terraforming Mars would begin in earnest, rebuilding the planet's lost atmosphere as it's population boomed. The Galilean moons of Jupiter would also become an important hub for Humanity. Io, in particular, became of extreme importance due to the discovery of Exousite in 2079. The amazing properties of Exousite soon made it the most valuable substance known to Humanity, revolutionizing energy production and storage in just a few short years.

Although it was initially valued for it's energy storage abilities, Exousite was soon discovered to have non-relativistic properties, able to warp space-time around it with sufficient energy. The implications were clear, and the UN soon established a scientific wing solely dedicated to unravelling the mystery of Exousite. The Einstein Project, as it would be known as, quickly became the driving force behind Humanity's technological development; and through it's ties with the UN, gave the organization even more political clout amongst the nations of Earth.

In 2096, the Einstein Project would achieve it's crowning glory: an unmanned probe sent five million kilometers through space in 16 seconds - travelling at 1.01% the speed of light. Soon afterwards, a manned UN vessel, the Relativity, was constructed and outfitted with a Spool Drive. Carrying a crew of four, the Relativity was able to safely make the typically weeks-long trip from Earth to Jupiter in just under 35 minutes.

Interstellar Expansion (2096-2160)

At the turn of the 22nd century, Earth was largely reliant on the resource colonies scattered throughout the solar system for the long recovery from the third world war. Understandably, breaking the light-barrier changed everything; a trip between Io - at this point the only known planetoid that contained Exousite - and Earth took over ten weeks with the fastest sub-light laser sailers. With a spool drive, that was cut to be under an hour. And in the late 2090s, the United Nations held a monopoly on the construction of FTL propulsion.

The first major step towards unifying the planet under a true world government was the creation of the International Spaceflight Regulatory Organization (ISRO) and United Nations Interstellar Exploratory Agency (UNIEA). The formation of these organization explicitly gave oversight and control over faster-than-light travel both within and outside of the Sol system to the UN from the various nation-states of Earth.

Formation of United Earth

With the UN now controlling the single most important piece of logistics in human history, the nations of Earth began conceding more and more centralization to the organization. On May 9th, 2108, the United Nations was dissolved into two separate organizations: United Earth, composed of roughly two-thirds of Earth's nation-states that voted to join into a single world-wide government; and the United Earth Associated Countries, made up of those that did not choose to join the new world government. The associated countries functioned very similarly to the previous United Nations, just with much of the organizations that composed it now a part of the United Earth government.

Within United Earth, the previous nation-states were largely left intact to ease the transition, and to govern their own territory - with some UE oversight and aid. Beyond the borders of the nation-states, United Earth organized trade both between it's constituent parts and with the associated countries; it maintained a small military force, particularly a (planetary) naval force to protect against piracy. Most off-world colonies had been founded by nation-states that joined the new world government, and management of them was thusly transferred to United Earth. Of note, the United States was not one of the initial members of United Earth, so it's major colonies on Europa remained separate - although still immensely reliant on UE's FTL travel.

By 2130, the last of the associated countries had joined United Earth; for the first time ever, all of Humanity was united under a single banner. Over the next few years, society on Earth was transformed as United Earth began to transition into a unitary government. The old borders between the nation-states were wiped away and replaced with a multitude of Federated Districts, each district containing approximately 30 million inhabitants - out of the planet's 11.2 billion people. Each district was given significant leeway on the day to day administration of its own territory, but was required to uphold certain principals set out by the unitary government.

The unitary constitution provided for an executive president as the head of state, elected by a popular vote and independent of the legislature. The president, in turn, was to appoint a prime minister that acted as the head of government, and served at the approval of the legislature. Forming a bicameral system; the upper house, known as the General Assembly (a direct successor from both the first and second United Nations), was composed of a single representative from each Federated District; while the lower house, the Delegate Assembly, where roughly half of the members are elected directly from constituencies within each Federated District, and the remaining seats being distributed based on proportional representation. On May 14th, 2133, the new constitution was fully ratified, properly establishing the Federate Districts and setting a timeframe for United Earth's first open elections the next year.

Early Colonization Efforts

Shortly after the organization of United Earth in 2108, a number of FTL capable exploration vessels were put down, and began deep space missions in 2111. The UNS Pytheas was given the honor of surveying the nearest star system to Sol: the trinary Alpha Centauri. By cosmic coincidence, Proxima Centauri b - the second planet of the red dwarf Proxima Centauri - boasted an oxygen-nitrogen atmosphere and temperature range suitable for Human life. Soon shortened to just [[Proxima]], the arid world would see it's first human colonists land in 2119.

First colonized in the second half of the 21st century, Earth's moon and Mars had both grown substantially over the past half century. Mars was particularly important - rich in titanium, aluminum, and other important elements - which combined with it's lower gravity and proximity to the similarly mineral rich asteroid belt made it a prime shipbuilding site. It would be at the Martian shipyards that most of United Earth's colonial, exploratory, and scientific fleet were be constructed.

Following the success of Proxima's discovery, the deep space survey missions were subject to great public interest, and would soon yield even more results. Tau Ceti, Epsilon Indi, Ran, Procyon, and 40 Eridani (commonly known as Keid) all contained habitable worlds that were near ideal for human comfort. Of these, Epsilon Indi would be the first to be colonized in 2126; the system's 3rd planet - a humid, roughly venus-sized world with 14 hour days - would be given the name Indira by it's settlers. Soon after, in 2130, with the United Earth government focused on overhauling it's political system; a joint venture was established with private interests to colonize Tau Ceti. It's world would be named after a fortress from an old Earth legend - Camelot.

Further away from Earth than the previously colonized worlds, the colonization of Yibbena - a habitable moon orbiting the large gas giant AEgir in the Ran system - in 2133 opened a new corner of the galaxy to Humanity. Yibbena quickly grew to become an important hub for the area, hosting the first large scale shipyards built outside of Sol allowed it to act as a springboard for the colonization of the Procyon and Keid systems. In Procyon, the planet Coriter was initially settled in 2137. Despite it's distance from Sol, Coriter ballooned in population relatively quickly compared to the other colonies - thanks in no small part to it's near perfectly habitable climate. Noticably larger in size, yet with roughly equal mass and gravity to Earth, Coriter was a lush, temperate world that was only slightly colder than Earth, on average.

The last major planet to be colonized under the United Earth government was Ophirion, in the Keid system in 2142. Unique to the other major colonized worlds in that it's colonists first came from Yibbena - itself not even a decade into settlement - rather than the Sol system. Within a few decades of the settlement of each of the major worlds, a divide in Human culture could already be noticed. The further flung, later-to-be-colonized systems to the galactic north of Sol began to form a distinct identity at the frontier of Human civilization; Ran, Procyon, and Keid formed the heart of what was soon referred to as the "Periphery" or the "Peripheral Worlds". Meanwhile, Earth, Mars, Indira, and Camelot, comprised the "Core Worlds" - containing the bulk of Humanity's population (especially within the Sol system, for obvious reasons).

Concurrently with the large-scale civilian colonization on the various major habitable worlds, various minor settlements were established across the Solar Neighborhood. Although not located on ideal, oxygen-nitrogen atmosphere, life-supporting, Earth-sized planets - these settlements were no less important to the fledgling Human civilization. Domed cities on barren moons, mazes buried deep within mineral rich asteroids, and sprawling space stations were built over the decades following the establishment of the United Earth government, and well into the years of its successor, the United Systems. These minor settlements were immensely reliant on the nearest major planet for their economy and many basic needs - most of them were focused on resource extraction, not growing crops. The necessity of travel between the nearby worlds furthered the distinction between the peripheral and core worlds of humanity.

Reformation into the United Systems

In the 2130s, Earth was severely overcrowded, it's population nearing the 12 billion mark. Within the solar system, large population growth was limited by the difficulty inherent of artificial habitats. Prior to Mars' terraforming, the only off-Earth colony who's population exceeded 10 million was Earth's moon.

So with the discovery of worlds that were naturally habitable to Human life

articles.title: United Equus
articles.content:
United Equus is the first unified world government of the planet [[Equus]], and the first truly interstellar government of the planet. It existed from the reorganization of the Convocation of Creatures into a planetary government in 1104 CYP until the formation of the Solar Empire in 1346 CYP.


United Equus was formed by the emergency resolution to reorganize the mostly diplomatic Convocation of Creatures into a legally binding planetary government following the outbreak of the Great War. Due to the nature of it's formation, there was need for a strong and decisive central government. Even following the end of the Great War and the demilitarization of the economy, the strong central government remained, a definite contrast to the other, decentralized members of the Sector Alliance. Despite this, there remained a high amount of cultural autonomy between the different species, regions, and colonies.

History

Pre-Unification

Prior to the Great War, Equus was made up of a collection of nation states, the most prominent being the Princessdom of Equestria and the Griffon Empire. Equestria became the dominant interstellar power of the planet, although cooperation between nations on interstellar projects was common. The Convocation of Creatures had been founded some many centuries prior to the beginning of Equus' interstellar era, though it mostly served as a diplomatic affair between nations and peoples. Due to rising international cooperation from the dawn of the space age, the Convocation began to take on a more active role in global affairs. Most notably, the committee that oversaw the colonization of Yildun Rest was an arm of the Convocation.

The Great War

The surprise attack on Equus' colony on Yildun Rest in 1104 CYP (2429 CE) was a galvanizing moment of national tragedy for the fledgling interstellar power. The Convocation of Creatures was called into an emergency session immediately after the attack, the assorted nation states unanimously voting to enter a state of war with the Apleen and Huachavi, and after some deliberation, the reorganization of the Convocation into a centralized global government on May 15th, 1104.

Princess Celestia of Equestria was selected as the first President of the Convocation, and in the following month the various systems of governance would be laid into place. Most notably was the formation of Equus High Command and the Equus Military Forces: initially the combined military assets of it's founding states, it would grow to become one of the major militaries of the region during the Great War. Shortly thereafter, the Sector Alliance was created between Equus, the United Systems Confederacy, and the Sarden Tribes, later adding the Preservers to the alliance.

Equus initially faired very poorly in the Great War, it's military was woefully inexperienced and under-equipped compared to their enemies. If not for the efforts and ability of the other members of the Sector Alliance, it is likely Equus would have lost the war almost immediately. Eventually though, the Equus Military Forces was able to grow and bring itself up to a comparable standard to other major interstellar powers. During the first half of the war, most of Equus' resources went into improving the military, including the construction of the ESS Eclipse.

Following the Battle of Caren Prime and the collapse of the Apleen civilian government, Equus was the strongest proponent of peace, believing the war to be essentially won. With the subsequent Zanden Conference and the formation of the Apleen Republic, Equus and the USC declared hostilities in the sector to be at an end, leading to the mutiny of the Continuation Forces and the fracturing of the Sector Alliance.

Although only a relatively small number of Equestrian ships and armies joined the Continuation Forces it was lead and initiated by the the immensely powerful ESS Eclipse and it's CO: the second highest ranking member of the Equus High Command, Admiral Nebula Bolt. The Equestrian Navy was tasked with hunting down and capturing Bolt and the Eclipse, along with the rest of the Continuation Forces, and clashed frequently, although the ESN was never successful in it's goal. Peace had been declared, but the Apleen fleet and Huachavi still considered the war ongoing, and although they primarily fought against the Continuation Forces, there were still many incidents of them fighting against Equestrian Forces.

Eventually, the Continuation Forces were able to destroy the last of the Apleen Fleet and force the Huachavi into surrender, marking the true end of the Great War. All Equestrian members of the Continuation Forces promptly surrendered to Equus, with Admiral Bolt bargaining the pardoning of the Continuation Forces in exchange for him pleading guilty of all charges levied against him. With the reintegration of the Continuation Forces, the Equus Military Forces demobilized for the first time in it's history, and the recovery of the Hydian Sector began.

Reconstruction

Despite the millions of lives lost during the Great War, Equestrian society and economy remained strong following demilitarization. Most reconstruction efforts were focused in the former disputed zone (now fully recognized Equestrian territory), and aid to their new ally, the Apleen Republic. The planet of Yildun Rest in particular, had suffered the complete loss of all infrastructure due to multiple fierce battles over the planet and a prolonged guerilla war.

As part of the early stages of reconstruction, a wave of legal proceedings occurred to legitimize the results of the war, most notably the war crime tribunal of Admiral Nebula Bolt. In a highly controversial ruling, Bolt was allowed to keep his rank and command, and sentence to never be allowed to step foot on Equus again, and would command the Eclipse as a patrol and training vessel in the furthest reaches of Equestrian territory up until it's disappearance in 2450.

As reconstruction waned, a wave of colonization picked up, spurred on by the development of the Las Pegasus II-class colony ship (the Las Pegasus I-class was designed to resettle war torn worlds like Yildun Rest). Many planets, especially in the former disputed zone, were settled during this time, including New Mustangia, Unicornis IV, and Clawrest.

The Hydian Peace

With belligerent powers like of Apleen DepSoc and Huachavi toppled and isolated respectively, the the various interstellar empires of the Hydian Sector entered a long period of prosperity, peace, and slow expansion commonly referred to as the Hydian Peace that lasted from ~2460 to the 2640s. Equus in particular finally became a societal equal to the other species of the area, as life on Equus during the Great War had remained stagnant, staying comparable to late 21st century Earth due to the focus towards the military.

With rapid societal advancement, a golden age of sorts was brought about on Equus, poverty and hunger were completely eliminated during the Hydian Peace, although such issues would rapidly reemerge following later instability. Equus' territory was expanded during this time, with 17 new colonies of various scales established across the stars, and countless mining and research outposts spread throughout.

The population of Equus and it's colonies rapidly expanded during the Hydian Peace; Equus, its moon, and assorted orbital habitats reaching 25 billion inhabitants by 2500. During this period of expansion, Equus incorporated the Sparkle Nebula, a stellar nursery, into its territory. The Sparkle Nebula would prove to be an immense boon to the burgeoning interstellar civilization, as it was extremely rich in natural materials.

The only time during the Hydian Peace that the EMF was mobilized was during the Ichizhou Realms in 2554, which fortunately saw no combat engagements. The crisis was brought on by the territorial expansion of Equus' ally, the Sarden Tribes, and the Sarden's historic rival, the Ichizhou Realms. As border clashes between the two threatened to bring the entire region into war once more, the Equestrian Navy was ordered into Sarden and USC territory along the border with the Ichizhou. The matter was settled peacefully by third party mediation from the Morden Union, which established a neutral zone between Sarden and Ichizhou territory, that prohibited any Sector Alliance or Ichizhou vessel from entering.

Infrastructure Construction

With the raw material of the Sparkle Nebula and the increased economic prosperity from the growing colonies, Equus undertook it's most ambitious project yet. Beginning in 2579, and completed 114 years later in the early years of the Solar Empire, the Olympus Ring was to be a gargantuan artificial ring encircling Equus. It would serve as a massive shipyard, the largest in the sector (the larger Caren Alpha Yards were destroyed during the Great War and wouldn't be rebuilt until after the fall of the Solar Empire). In addition to the shipyard, it would be an orbital habitat for millions of inhabitants, and a star port that facilitated the rapid transport of material to and from the planet's surface.

Most analysts agree that the reckless spending and rampant government corruption that occurred during construction the Olympus Ring is what began the social and political instability that marked the decline of the Hydian Peace for Equus. Frequently behind schedule and over budget, it's plans twisted by various officials and private citizens for their own gain, the incomplete ring above the planet began to draw ire from the general population, who rightfully believed that funds originally intended for social causes had gone into the Ring.

End of the Hydian Peace

Starting in the 2590s, Equus began to enter a period of social, economic, and political decline; spurred on by the construction of the Ring. Social spending was slashed during this time, leading to the reemergence of poverty on Equus, only adding more fuel to the fire. Minor protests flared up on various worlds during the period between 2590 and 2653, until they reached a head with the Puerto Paraíso Scandal. The result of decades of rising government corruption, the scandal began as an instance of simple real-estate fraud on the resort moon of Puerto Paraíso, but investigations quickly uncovered a whole bevy of other, much more sinister crimes that led all the way up the government ladder. The current president, Gucharo Picorroca, was found guilty in multiple crimes related to the scandal, becoming the only President of the Convocation to ever be imprisoned. Following Picorroca's trial, his entire cabinet, along with his successor, and 56 members of the Convocation all resigned their posts in a last ditch attempt to avoid justice.

In an attempt to restore faith in the government, Convocation member Princess Celestia was selected as the interim president. Celestia had routinely been an immensely popular figure; Prior to her appointment as interim president, she had served 15 consecutive and non-consecutive terms as President. Celestia was properly elected president shortly thereafter, winning a landslide victory the likes of which hadn't been seen since the Great War.

Despite Celestia's crusade against government corruption, tensions still remained high, as she was unable to fully alleviate the socio-economic issues that had begun to show up in the prior decades. In addition to the social issues, external problems were rampant during the 2630s. Due to the ecological collapse on the Janset colony brought about by overhunting of native fauna, multiple Sarden Tribes took to a life of piracy, primarily raiding into Equestrian Space. Additionally, the Huachavi finally ended their long period of isolationism and began to antagonize the Sector Alliance through subversive means.

All the issues brewing under the surface of Equestrian society reached a head in 2666 with the Assassination of Princess Cadence. The then President Celestia took the death of her neice hard, and began pushing harder and harder to find the perpetrators of the assassination. Any suspects were rounded up and held without trial, alongside many more gross violations of the rule of law. Eventually, the investigation determined that the attack was carried out by Huachavi operatives with the blessing of the Huachavi government.

Equus immediately called for war against the Huachavi, but the other members of the Sector Alliance refused to join, citing the multitude of holes in the investigation's finding. Because of this perceived betrayal, Equus formally exited the Sector Alliance in 2668. They would be followed by the fractured Sarden Tribes and the Preservers in 2669 and 2670 respectively, leaving only the USC and the Apleen Republic.

Instability and Reformation

Following the exit from the Sector Alliance, xenophobia on Equus and her colonies ran rampant, and violent hate crimes against aliens - especially against Huachavi - were common. In March of 2670, the Manehattan Race Riots broke out in Equus' most diverse city, and grew to such a scale the necessitated the deployment of the Equus Planetary Forces. During the riots, Celestia was granted emergency powers in order to bring order to the city, and to prevent any other large scale protests. The rioters in Manehattan were crushed, but the Convocation didn't bother to end Celestia's emergency powers, instead extending the state of emergency until Celestia herself saw fit to declare it over.

A year later, in 2671, a reports of a skirmish above the Equestrian Nekmer colony (A system that had been annexed from the Huachavi at the conclusion of the Great War) took Equus in a frenzy. This so-called "Battle of Nekmer" supposedly saw a small Huachavi task force enter the system in an attempt to occupy the colony, but were beaten back by the brave Equestrian defenders. To date, no concrete evidence that any Huachevi armed assets were present or even near the Nekmer system at the recorded date of the battle - it is widely believed by modern scholars that the "Battle" was a either false flag attack orchestrated covertly by the Equus military, or a complete fabrication by Celestia's government.

Nonetheless, the public outcry over the precieved attack turned against the last remaining opposition to Celestia's rule. Those that didn't support her were blamed for "allowing" the attack to happen, and any remaining opposition Convocation members were purged from the body. Through it all, Celestia was increasingly viewed as the guilding beacon of stability that kept Equus safe, until the Convocation (now completely controlled by the pro-Celestia camp) declared her the "Solar Empress" of Equus, and formalized her emergency powers into complete autocracy. Following the proclamation of the Solar Empire, the Convocation disbanded itself permanently, completely passing all governmental power to the newly christend Empress Celestia.

articles.title: United Systems Confederacy
articles.content:
The United Systems Confederacy (USC) was the dominant Human government from the late 23rd century to the 28th century. While not the first interstellar government of the Human race, the USC stands as the longest lasting and most impactful. It is not to be confused with the similarly named [[United Systems]], the government that preceded it.

The United Systems Confederacy was formed by the Fomalhaut Declaration: the reunification of the peripheral and core systems that ended the United Systems Civil War. The civil war is often considered Humanity's darkest hour: the closest it has come to complete extinction, with the destruction of the 8 major Human-inhabited worlds. In the aftermath, the belligerent parties set aside their differences for the sake of survival, forming the USC. Initially based on Skagmore, in the Fomalhaut system, it was under the USC that [[Terra Astra]] was discovered and settled as a new homeworld for Humanity. From their new home, Humanity expanded into a major regional power in the Hydian Sector. During the early 25th century, the USC became a founding member of the Sector Alliance following the outbreak of the Great War. The USC remained one of the final members of the Sector Alliance, up until it's annexation by the Solar Empire.

History

Background

Pre-FTL (Until 2096)

The roots of the USC can be traced back to the first United Nations (UN) on [[Earth]], established by the victors of the second of Earth's devastating World Wars. The first UN existed for a little under 100 years, from the mid 20th century until the early 21st century. During the final years of the first UN, Humanity first set foot on a planet other than earth when astronauts from the United States of America (a major nation-state of the era) landed on [[Mars]]. Beginning in the 2020s, major tensions began to flare between the various nation-states of Earth, resulting in the dissolution of the first UN in 2035.

Despite it's important nature as the ancestor to the USC, the first UN is remembered by historians as being toothless and ineffectual, powerless to stop the third of Earth's World Wars from breaking out. World War 3, as it was contemporarily known, was a brief but extremely devastating conflict: the deadliest war in Human history up to that point. Beginning in 2039, it was initially confined to conventional warfare on the continents of Europe and Asia, the war eventually escalated into a mutually destructive nuclear exchange. Between the direct effects of the exchange, the resulting fallout, and nuclear winter, it is estimated that around 25-30% of the planet's population was killed.

Following the complete upheaval wrought by the end of World War 3, the nation-states of Earth came together to rebuild Humanity, and to ensure such a terrible conflict would never happen again. Established by the Lagos Accords in 2054, the second United Nations would steward Humanity throughout the rest of the 21st century. The lasting effects of WW3 caused a multitude of issues on Earth, spurring on space exploration. It was during this rush into space that many early colonies in the Sol system were established: [[Mars]] initially as a way to get away from radiation plaguing earth, Titan and the Jovian moons as mining colonies. Io in particular would soon become of extreme importance to the growing Human civilization, with the discovery of Exousite on the moon in 2079. Due to issues on Earth, there were attempts to seek out a new planet for humanity, using slower-than-light sleeper ships. One of these sleeper ship fleets, while en route to Alpha Centauri, disappeared, transported across the galaxy, and later becoming founding members of the Domedeus Alliance, unbeknownst to the rest of humanity. During this period as well, the second UN's role as a centralized government expanded, especially concerning extraterrestrial matters. It was under a UN program that Humanity developed their first faster than light drive in 2096; an early form of the Spool Drive that utilized primitively refined exousite as it's main focal material.

United Earth and United Systems (2096-2265)

As the reality of FTL set in on Humanity, it quickly became apparent that division between the dozens of nation-states was ineffective and detrimental to Humanity as a whole. The second UN, having expanded it's authority throughout the second half of the 21st century, was reorganized into the United Earth government in 2108. Carrying over much of the same principals of the second UN, United Earth's early existence was consumed by the mundane yet arduous task of simply organizing a worldwide government. During these early years, Human explorers fanned out across the stars, charting the space around Sol; importantly, a world much like Earth was discovered in orbit Alpha Centuari. Named [[Proxima]], it was more arid than Earth, but nonetheless well suited for human habitation with minimal terraforming.

The colonization of Proxima in 2119 was the first great triumph of the United Earth, soon to be followed by settlments in other systems. Epsilon Indi in 2126, Tau Ceti in 2130, Ran in 2133, Procyon in 2137, and Keid in 2142; these systems hosted the most desirable worlds for Human life, and would quickly become major pillars to Human civilization. It was during this period of rapid expansion that Humanity would meet it's first extraterrestrial civilization: the Saventi. After picking up unknown signals emanating from the 61 Cygni system, the exploratory vessel UES Zheng He encountered a Saventi space station located in the system. Despite a friendly first contact, the Saventi remained insular, allowing little trade with the burgeoning Human civilization.

As the colonies grew, those living on the settled worlds other than earth began to demand representation in the government. Spearheaded by the efforts of Mars, Proxima, and Coriter, the primary planet of Procyon, United Earth expanded equal status to the seven major worlds other than Earth itself, and provided a framework to ascend future colonies to equal status. With this reform, United Earth became known officially as the [[United Systems]] in 2160. Although all constituent worlds were in theory meant to have equal status in the United Systems, in practice Earth still remained dominant for most the next hundred years, simply due to it's population and economic hegemony. Under the United Systems, many more systems were settled by Humanity, including Fomalhaut, Altair, and Sirius. As the colonies grew in population, they began to develop a unique cultural identity, especially in Procyon, Ran, and Keid; what would become known as the Periphery, due to their distance from Earth and the other "core systems": The older and more economically prosperous systems, such as Alpha Centauri and Tau Ceti. It is during this period of general peace and quiet expansion that Humanity and the Apleen made first contact. Their relationship remained cool, with the Apleen highly protective of their territory, but remained isolationist.

It was from this culture divide between the settled world that instability would begin to grow within the United Systems. The core worlds domineered trade within the United Systems, often at the expense of local interests within the Periphery. Due to growing resentment towards core world traders, and exacerbated by an economic downturn in the periphery throughout the 2230s, the Ran Belt Riots erupted in late 2238, lasting into 2239 until they were put down by the central government. The heavy handed approach used to quell the riots galvanized and angered many citizens of the Periphery, who turned to more violent measures to voice their dissent. It was from this insurrection that the United Systems began to militarize itself, not since the days of World War 3 had human civilization been so armed. As the central government was unable to effectively combat the insurrection in the Periphery, the governments of the Peripheral Systems were allowed to maintain local forces of their own, to support the central government's efforts. The insurgency dragged on for over a decade, until 2251, when a raid by central government forces left 27 innocent citizens of the periphery dead in what would be called the Dalton City Massacre.

Following the massacre, Procyon declared it's secession from the United Systems, soon taking the rest of the Periphery with it, and sparking full-blown conflict between the Peripheral Systems and the Core Worlds in the United Systems Civil War. The Peripheral Systems of Procyon, Ran, Keid, and associated minor colonies turned the militaries allowed to them to fight the insurgents against the core worlds, especially Earth. Alpha Centauri and Tau Ceti soon threw in with Earth, with Epsilon Indi remaining neutral for much of the conflict. As Humanity had never fought a sustained, large scale interstellar conflict, the early phase of the war was slow going for both sides, with limited or ill fated strikes against the opposing side as they race to properly militarize. Conventional warfare reached a peak in the mid 2250s, with large space battles defining most of the war. Planetary and ground based engagements proved to be extremely difficult for both sides; the largest colony fully occupied by either side was the small, barren moon of Talanpir, in the Sirus system, with only a few hundred thousand inhabitants scattered across various domed cities. The reasoning behind this lack of success in such a key theater of war is attributed to an equilibrium in military technology, akin to the Earth's first World War. As attempts at invasions repeatedly failed or stagnated by either side, by the late 2250s and into 2260, it seemed as if the war was winding down, neither side having made much inroads at all.

That is, until the complete destruction of Earth in 2261. Now known to be caused by an ancient extraterrestrial monster known only as "The Ruin", the speed at which it happened, and the comparatively primitive sensor technology of the time, resulted in the true cause being unknown until the era of the Solar Empire, long after Earth's destruction. Contemporaries, especially those of the Core Worlds, were quick to put the blame on the Peripheral Systems, who's denial fell on deaf ears. With the loss of Earth, the civil war took a darker turn: no longer was the war over secession or cultural divide, but a matter of life and death; the core worlds became convinced that whatever the Peripheral Systems had (supposedly) used to destroy Earth would be used again. The stalemate that had settled over the war for the previous decade would first be broken by the United Systems, when they began orbital nuclear bombardment of Yibbena, in the Ran system. In a matter of hours, Yibbena's surface was razed, killing all of it's inhabitants, and ending the years long siege it had been under. In response, Tau Ceti suffered a similar fate to Yibbena at the hands of the Peripheral Systems. The stalemate between the two sides had been broken, at the cost of uncountable of lives. Keid and Mars would be the next two worlds destroyed, again by nuclear bombardment, all while an arms race began between two sides in order to develop more effective planet-killers.

The Core Worlds, now just Alpha Centauri and Epsilon Indi (who joined against the Periphery after the destruction of Earth), won the race, creating a device that could disrupt a planet's natural magnetic field. Coriter was the natural target, it's atmosphere was stripped by solar winds and it's inhabitants irradiated by the full might of Procyon. With all their major planets gone, the Peripheral Systems launched one, final, desperate attempt: their rival superweapon, codenamed by it's creators as "Fog Machine", was smuggled onto Proxima. The Fog Machine began to covert the air itself into poison, that spread across the planet in a just four hours, causing any living being caught by it a prolonged, agonizing death. The relatively slow spread of the fog enabled much of the population to evacuate, which soon became the true killing blow to the United Systems. One of the final ships to make it off world had a compromised hull, enabling a minute amount of the weapon to spread into the ship, after it's course had already been set to Epsilon Indi. With nobody alive to guide it's landing, it crashed onto the surface, releasing the fog onto the final major world of Humanity. In just four years since 2261, Humanity had lost all of their major worlds, and billions upon billions of lives lost in a war that nobody truly won.

Foundation and Expansion (2265-2429)

With the end to the fighting, Humanity was at the brink of extinction - it's largest world left was Skagmore, in Fomalhaut, a near-barren, harsh world that was ill suited for the large influx of refugees from both sides of the war. It was on that world though that the civil war would be brought to a formal close, the survivors of both sides agreeing to set aside their difference for the sake of survival against annihilation. The Fomalhaut Declaration simultaneously ended the conflict and dissolved both the United Systems and Peripheral Systems, merging into the United Systems Confederacy. The Confederacy was meticulously crafted to ensure nothing like the civil war would happen again; it set up a much less powerful central government, with every single inhabited area of humanity recieving representation in the new Confederate Congress, which in turn had a series of checks and balances to ensure no single system or party could dominate the others.

Perhaps more pressing than establishing a new government, however, was the matter of finding a new home for humanity, as Skagmore and the remaining smaller colonies were unsuited to sustain a large population for any extended period of time. As such, much of humanity took the stars in nomad fleets, wandering from system to system, searching for a new home. It is from these nomads that much of the Human diaspora across the galaxy can trace their roots to. Many fleets went towards the galactic east, towards Saventi space, some settling there and some pushing onwards. Those that continued on would eventually reunite with the long lost Human sleeper ship colonists in the Domedeus Alliance, who's descendents had become the Archeri through genetic engineering.

It was the groups that struck out to the galactic south, though, that had the most lasting effect on Human Civilization - it was them, who pushed into what they called the Hydian Reaches, that would discover [[Terra Astra]]. Almost identical to the Earth in all regards, save for it's noticeably larger size, Terra Astra was the home Humankind had been looking for since the end of the civil war. Efforts to organize a massive resettlement began as soon as word reached Skagmore, but soon ran into issues; notably, the most direct route between Fomalhaut and the newly designated Nova Sol was through Apleen space. The Apleen, although they paid little attention to Humanity beforehand, were highly territorial, destroying multiple colony ships as they unknowingly passed into Apleen space. The USC, unable to put up a fight, opted instead to take a longer route, via 12 Ophiuchi and Sacoren's Star, but relations between them and the Apleen had been permanently damaged, and the two would become fierce rivals over the course of the next 200 years.

The Great War (2429-2444)
