articles.title: Ichizhou Empire
articles.content: The Ichizhou Empire was the primary government of the Ichizhou from the unification of their homeworld, [[Airehai]], until it's eventual annexation by the [[Solar Empire]]. One of the oldest interstellar polities of the Hydian Sector, the empire underwent several periods of decline and regrowth over it's several hundred year history, most notably between the second half of the 24th century and the early 26th century.

articles.title: Equus Star Navy
articles.content:
The Equus Star Navy was one of the the main service branches of Equus Military Forces, designated as overseeing all space-capable military vessels and space-to-space marine operations. Following the Great War, it emerged as one of the main space forces of the Hydian Sector.

 

The ESN was converted into Imperial Navy in 1346 CYP (2671 CE)

History

Background

The need for a unified Equus was quickly realized following the 1st Battle of Yildun Rest and the start of the Great War, leading to the Convocation of Creatures voting unanimously to form a world government on May 15th, 2429 CE. This decision did not immediately include any armed forces, leaving command of all space vessels to the pre-established, civilian Equestrian Space Agency (ESA). A month later, on June 14th, the Equus High Command and the Equus Military Force (EMF) are formalized, transferring all military assets into two main branches: the Equus Planetary Forces (EPF) and the Equus Star Navy (ESN). The ESN received all of the ESA's fleet, barring only the ESAV Starswirl. Prism Gleam and Nebula Bolt were designated as the ESN's the first ranking admirals, with Prism Gleam appointed as the commander-in-chief of the EMF.

The Great War

Within the first 6 months of the Great War, the majority of the fleet (consisting of mostly Manehattan-Class  and Cloudsdale I-class ships) had been destroyed or rendered unusable. The ESN, along with most of the rest of the Sector Alliance, was forced to withdraw most of it's forces away from the front line for much needed upgrades and repair, including the installation of advanced Preserver shield technology. During this period, the first mainline combat vessel would be launched: the ESS Cloudsdale-A, the first Cloudsdale II-class Cruiser. The Cloudsdale II-Class would quickly become the backbone of the Equus Navy for the majority of the Great War. The next few years would see the introduction of more major ship classes: the Fillydelphia-Class Escort in 2430, the Canterlot-Class Command Cruiser in 2431, and the Baltimare-Class Escort and Vanhoover-Class Carrier in 2433.

articles.title: Solar Empire
articles.content:
The Solar Empire was a autocratic empire that lasted from 2671 CE (Year 0 in the Imperial Calendar) to 2962 (Solar Year 291). It was based on the planet of Equus and and covered about one-tenth of the galaxy at it's height. It was formed out of the [[United Equus]] government following a prolonged period of instability, when the then President Celestia was declared to be the Eternal Empress of Equus and it's colonies.

The Solar Empire was an aggressive hegemon, in a constant cycle of expansion and conquest of it's neighbors. The Empire was also highly xenophobic, and practiced a policy of species-based superiority, with all non-Equestrian races living at best as second-class citizens, and at worse chattel slaves. Despite this, the Empire was relatively stable and prosperous for most of it's existence, thanks in no small part to the overwhelming superiority of the Imperial military. During it's final decades, the Empire began to face progressively more serious internal threats, most notably the prolonged guerilla war of Luna's Rebellion. Ultimately, the Solar Empire would fall in the Battle of Equus after the reemergence of the ESS Eclipse and the Nightmare Fleet.

History

Background

Main article: [[United Equus]] 

From the mid-25th into the early 27th centuries, the Hydian Sector enjoyed a period of relative peace and prosperity, known as the Hydian Peace. However - beginning in the 2590s - United Equus and the other Hydian powers entered a prolonged social, economic, and political decline. Particularly, United Equus faced a resurgence of poverty, which had been effectively wiped out during the Hydian Peace, due to and economic downturn spurred on by massive government corruption. In the fallout of the Puerto Paraíso Scandal of 2653, many government officials, including the president at the time, were found guilty of multiple crimes.

In the aftermath of the scandal, Celestia - Princess of Equestria and previous president of United Equus on multiple occasions - was appointed as interim president, and was soon re-elected in her own right in a landslide victory. Within two terms, President Celestia managed to curtail government corruption and overhaul the bureaucracy that had slowly inflated to unsustainable proportions over the centuries. Despite these successes, United Equus began to face serious external issues at around the same time; the Huachavi ended their long-term isolationism and began to antagonize the Sector Alliance once more. Additionally, many Sarden tribes had turned to a life of piracy, frequently marauding over the border into Equestrian space.

It was the suspicious death of Celestia's own niece, Princess Cadence, that began to push Equus over the precipice. The Huachavi were blamed for the assassination, and Equus called their allies to war, to which they were rebuffed - due to concerns raised about the validity of the rushed investigation. In the wake of this perceived betrayal, the Sector Alliance quickly collapsed, with Equus, the Sarden, and the Preservers leaving the alliance. Xenophobia began to run rampant in Equestria space, culminating in the disastrous Manehattan Race Riots of 2670, during which Celestia was granted emergency powers with the consent of the Convocation of Creatures (United Equus' governing legislature).

These emergency powers were only to be used during a declared state of emergency, which only the President, Celestia herself, had the authority to lift. With the backing of military leadership, President Celestia never lifted the state of emergency that had been declared by the race riots. A year later in 2671, the Nekmer colony came under assault - supposedly by Huachavi forces (although many modern scholars speculate it was a false flag attack by elements of the Equus military) - but the attack was repulsed by the Equestrian defenders. This so-called "Battle of Nekmer" was the final straw needed for total erosion of United Equus' democracy, and soon after Celestia was declared as the "Eternal Solar Empress of Equus and it's Colonies" - a moment that is marked by historians as the foundation of the Solar Empire.

Creation of an Empire

Early Years and Initial Conflicts

1st Huachavi Border War

Main article: 1st Equestrian-Huachavi Border War 

This new empire was born into conflict; immediately following her ascension, Empress Celestia ordered a punitive invasion of the Huachavi. This brief, two year border war was not the resounding victory the Empire had hoped for: the new Imperial military was in the process of reorganizing and purging itself of any would-be subversive elements, leading to substantial chaos and miscommunication on the battlefront. The true victor of the conflict, however, was Celestia's rapidly influential propaganda machine. The general public knew little of the failures of the war, state-run media simply extolling the bravery of the Empress' forces and declaring that justice against the Huachavi had been served.

As stated above, a number of political purges were executed during the border war, causing a severe drain on veteran officers, sailors, and soldiers. Certain authoritarian-leaning elements of the military had largely been responsible for the rise of the empire, and quickly rose to a position of dominance over the military as a whole. These elements were eager to clean house in order to secure their positions at the top by removing perceived "disloyal" or "untrustworthy" officers that had dutifully served under old government. Re-education and recruitment of newer, more impressionable servicemembers enforced the growing imperial dogma, but the purges still weighed heavily on the effectiveness of the Solar Empire's military in the 1st border war.

The Marauder War

Main article: Marauder War 

With an expanded and refreshed military, and a population still hungry for justice against perceived alien aggressors, the empire began looking for yet another conflict - and in 2677, spurred on by decades of deadly raids, Imperial warships crossed the border into Sarden space. The goal, unlike the hasty and unfocused entry into the Huachavi border war, was clear: destroy any hostile Sarden marauders and neutralize their bases to prevent more from filling the gap. Due to the decentralized nature of the Sarden tribes, this was not a war against the Sarden as a whole, just those tribes that had turned to piracy and threatened Equestrian citizens and interests. In fact, the empire were not the only victims of the piratical tribes' violence; many other Sarden tribes suffered from raids and wanted to see them gone just as much, if not more, than the Solar Empire. On some occasions, imperial forces were even joined by opportunistic tribes, some truly wanting to help end the pirate threat, some just wanting to settle old blood feuds between clans.

Janset III, the pirate "homeworld" was captured relatively quickly in the war, with brutal reprisals against an Sarden suspected of aiding or engaging in piracy. Other pirate bases were similarly quickly handled; the initial sweep into Sarden space taking less than a year to destroy and capture all major sources power of the marauding clans. Actually hunting down and eliminating the pirates themselves, however, proved to be a different story - parts of the imperial navy would remain in active duty in the area for over a decade.

The operation as a whole though, was a resounding success; despite the last hold-outs lasting many years past the initial invasion, Sarden marauders from this area would never again threaten imperial space. Additionally, the occupied territory was soon annexed into the Solar Empire - it's first true conquest - with little political opposition. After all, the clans that previously controlled the space and people within it were pirates, reviled by all their neighbors, including other Sarden.

2nd Huachavi Border War

Main article: 2nd Equestria-Huachavi Border War 

In the first border conflict with the Huachavi, Equestrian success amounted to seizing a few near-worthless systems, many of which remained under informal occupation by the Solar Empire for years afterwards. Aiming to recapture their lost territories, the Huachavi - seizing on the opportunity presented by the ongoing Marauder War - attacked imperial border defenses in 2678. By 2679, the previously lost systems were back in in Huachavi hands, with a few imperial systems occupied in return. These victories lead the Huachavi to perceive the Solar Empire as weak, especially as the Marauder War dragged on longer and longer in Sarden space.

Seizing on the opportunity, the Huachavi launched an attack on the Nekmer system, which they had previously ceded control of after the Great War, but were met by a far larger imperial naval force than expected. On the imperial side, the Marauder War had entered its second phase, and far less military assets were needed in Sarden space than the previous year. Much of the fleet was transferred to the Huachavi front, and had been massing at Nekmer when the Huachavi attack came. Although the Huachavi were caught off guard, the battle was hard fought for both sides, but the imperial force managed to destroy more Huachavi ships than they themselves lost, leading to a Huachavi retreat.

The 2nd Battle of Nekmer was the first large scale engagement of the Solar Empire, and had ended in a victory that sent their main threat retreating far back into Huachavi territory. With the Huachavi border forces out of the picture for a moment, both the occupied imperial systems and lost gains from the first border war were quickly seized once more. Imperial forces began pushing deeper into Huachavi space, occupying more minor systems until the two fleets met again in the Stexuipec system. The Battle of Stexuipec was much of a repeat of the previous engagement at Nekmer: an imperial victory, although not one without losses, and the Huachavi forced into retreat.

With two consecutive losses, and unwilling to commit to a larger engagement, the Huachavi called for peace talks in mid 2680. Despite the mutual mistrust between the two species, an agreement was reached that was largely in the Solar Empire's favor: The Huachavi would recognize the empire's annexation of the most of the currently occupied border territories, with the remaining occupied systems, along with some un-occupied systems from the Huachavi side, would form a neutral strip between the two where neither military was allowed to enter.

Imperialization

Throughout both Huachavi border wars, civilian society within the Empire was in the process of transitioning away from the democratic rule and systems of United Equus. One of the first major reforms was the Demographic Identification Act - which began the process of curtailing the rights of non-Equestrians within the Empire, even those who had, by this point, come from generations of Equus citizens. Under the act, any non-Equestrian species within the borders of the Empire was required to keep a state issued ID with them at all times, and submit to checks of said ID in various situations that covered every aspect of life, from shopping to travel to doctor's appointments. This allowed the government to keep track of the movements and activities of practically every alien in the Empire at all times.

To go alongside the new host of laws issued by the new empire, various arms of government were created or expanded, such as the aforementioned state propaganda machine. Equus' military had been a key part in enabling the rise of the Solar Empire, and as such many of these new functions of government fell within the direct purview of the powerful imperial military. The Military Office for Internal Security (MOfIS, or simply Internal Security), was one of these militarized branches, which was initially responsible for keeping track of the empire's alien population through the Demographic Identification Act.

While civil society and government reorganized itself, the Imperial military also underwent extensive changes from it's United Equus predecessor - especially after the failures in the 1st Huachavi border war. Mandatory military service was implemented in 2682, providing a continual supply of soldiers and sailors for the ongoing Marauder War and all future conflicts. Changes and exemptions to conscription would be created throughout the Empire's existence; most notably an option (for Equestrian races only) to waive military service and instead join a colonial charter. Although Celestia was nominally in command of the empire and it's military, it was the highest generals and admirals (many of which were directly responsible for the events that led to the rise of the empire) that often dictated policy for the first few decades of the Solar Empire.

Early Imperial Era

Internal Improvements

Long considered a symbol of Equus' economic and political decline, the Olympus Ring still sat unfinished above Equus even in the early years of the Solar Empire. Soon after her ascension, without the old legislature to get in the way of funding, Empress Celestia directed much of the Empire's funding to the rapid completion of the ring. To placate military leadership after using money that was initially earmarked for the navy, it much of the planned civilian-oriented sections of the ring were changed to expanded shipyards and other military elements, such as training and logistical facilities.

In 2693, 114 years after construction began, the Olympus Ring would be officially completed. During the empire's rush to finish construction, a new generation of warships had been designed; and during the final stages of building the ring, the first such warship, the ISS Phoenix, was built concurrently in an already complete shipyard section of the ring. A grand celebration was held to commemorate the ring's completion, which included a flyover of Equus' major cities by the Phoenix. Both Celestia and the citizens of Equus saw the event as a changing of eras: the decay and corruption of United Equus was now well and truly over, replaced by the efficient and prosperous Solar Empire.

Military Autonomy

Pro-imperial factions within the military had been instrumental to the initial rise of the Solar Empire; and a small group of senior admirals, generals, and business leaders had used the earlier conflicts to solidify their grip over military policy and autonomy from the Empress' commands. While Celestia's rule was strengthened by the stability provided by the military, it was this cabal of senior leadership that benefited the most from this arrangement. Granted a massive budget, mandatory civilian service, and the prodigious Olympus Ring shipyards completed, the Empire's military was stronger than ever before during the 2690s.

However, this burgeoning military force sat unused at peace-time. Most of the space force was dedicated to simple border operations along the Huachavi and Sarden frontiers; and the planetary forces were much too large to justify dedicating all of it to the occupation of integration of Janset III and other minor annexed planetoids. An outlet was needed for such a large and power-hungry military force. Amongst the civil populace, there was little appetite for a third minor war with the Huachavi; or worse, conflict with Equus' former allies whom much of the population still had some fleeting fondness for.

Occupation of Drakan

Main article: Occupation of Drakan

In 2696, an Equestrian deep-space observation post monitoring the pre-FTL civilization on the planet Drakan—located within the Thraskir system—reports a major plague has begun to spread among the native Drak'vorr people. The plague, today known to have been engineered by elements within the military leadership, was projected to kill most, if not all, of the Drak'vorr. Under the auspices of "rescuing" the species, Imperial planetary forces descended upon the world, seizing communications hubs and government centers of the fractured, industrializing Drak'vorr nation-states. Drakan was firmly within Imperial control in under a week; any token resistance was dealt with swiftly, and the drip-fed promises of a cure kept most of the population in line.

There is some historical debate as to how much of the occupation plans Empress Celestia was aware of prior to troops landing on Drakan. The common consensus holds that she was most likely unaware of the bioengineered nature of the plague, and likely would not have ordered a prolonged occupation at that point in her reign. However, it is believed she was strong-armed into supporting the military's occupation plans in order to preserve her standing with the civilian population. What is true is that her administration leaned hard into the narrative of Equestrian superiority: that the Drak'vorr were too primitive to have been able to save themselves, arguing that the Empire was simply bringing enlightenment to the poor species.

Despite the view of the Ministry of Information, the occupation of Drakan proved to be anything but a mission of enlightenment. Once the cure had been distributed, the Drak'vorr began to push for the end of the occupation, and the Empire responded by tightening its grip. The Drak'vorr became subject to the Demographic Identification Act and the Office of Alien Affairs, treating them as second class citizens on their own homeworld. Soon, organized resistance began: spiraling into a decade long bloodbath that left thousands on both sides dead. While the Drak'vorr fought adamantly, the technological superiority and numbers of Imperial forces would put an end to any organized revolution after a decade of fighting in 2709.

The blood-soaked occupation would be a watershed moment for Empress Celestia and her sister, Princess Luna. Luna, at the time governor of her namesake moon, protested the actions of the military on Drakan as an overreaction, and urged Celestia to seek peaceful resolution. In Luna's historical account, her sister disagreed, believing that a firm grip was necessary to achieve stability. As Celestia argued, such had been proven by the foundation and success of the Solar Empire, and it's dealing with past hostility in the Marauder War. Although a hard split between the two would not occur until much later in the Empire's history, scholars agree that the occupation of Drakan is where the sisters path's began to diverge.

Military Autonomy

Pro-imperial factions within

Organization

Government Structure

As a unitary state, power was ultimately vested within Empress Celestia, though the degree of her control over the empire shifted over time. While the Solar Empire had no formal legislature at any point in its history, various advisory councils or de facto legislative groups existed at multiple points in it's history. Empress Celestia also served as commander-in-chief of the Imperial Military, which itself held important political sway. Rule over individual planets, systems, and sectors* was given over to various governors, who served at the approval of the Empress. Many of these governorships developed into hereditary rule or aristocracies, as Celestia appointed successive governors from the same trusted family or families.

The judicial branch also served at the behest of Celestia, who could appoint and remove judges and tribunes at will. During the latter half of the Solar Empire's existence, Imperial Censors became increasingly powerful: serving as both prosecutor and judge, especially when it came to political prisoners and non-Equestrian citizens. Trial by jury existed in a limited fashion within the empire, restricted to the lower regional and local courts, and generally only for Equestrians. In the event that two higher courts disagree, or a case is contested, the case would be taken to the Empress for her personal judgement.

* Not to be confused with modern day Republic sectors. Imperial sectors were much smaller and consisted of only a handful of star systems

Administration

Executive Governorships

The day to day affairs of the various planets and systems within the Solar Empire was largely handed over to Executive Governors, while the Empress directed national policy. Governorships typically corresponded to population, rather than physical astrography. For example, despite their extreme proximity to one another, Equus and its moon, Luna, had separate Executive Governors. Meanwhile, the largest governorships might span hundreds of light-years across, yet only contain a handful of scattered settlements and stations.

Although overall control of the military fell under the Empress (especially when relating to actions against other powers), Executive Governors were expected to maintain force projection and the imperial order within their own territory. Most notably, this included anti-piracy actions, as well as maintaining law enforcement among the population. This duty meant that Executive Governors were often appointed from the military. With rare exceptions, law enforcement organizations within the empire were heavily militarized, and especially so in areas with high non-equestrian populations.

The first Executive Governors were largely handpicked by the early imperial military leadership, who had been instrumental in the establishment of the Solar Empire as a whole. These early governors were typically more loyal to the admiral or general who had sponsored them than to Celestia - in some cases the governors were the military leaders. However, as the first generation of military leadership died off - and as Celestia consolidated her power - many of the old governors were dismissed and quickly replaced by those more loyal to the Empress. Notably, Celestia's own sister: Princess Luna, was given the governorship of her namesake moon.

Bureaucracy

Main article: Governmental Departments of the Solar Empire 

One of the earliest and most important changes from the previous United Equus government into the Solar Empire was a complete gutting and overhaul of the bureaucracy. Equus' bureaucracy was at best inefficient, and - at worst - massively corrupt. The empire's new bureaucracy began as a split system: a civilian side, presided over by Celestia herself; and a military side, initially de facto presided over by the high command, despite legally falling under the Empress' purview as well. As the empire developed, and as Celestia consolidated control around herself and away from the high command, these two branches became intertwined with one another.

articles.title: Planetary Classifications
articles.content: The DCRA Astrological Survey utilizes planetary classifications to organize planets and planetoids. Various factors are taken into account when classifying a planet, including atmospheric composition, magnetic field, surface temperature, vegetation, and size. Single letter classifications make up base classes, with multi-letter classifications occasionally being designated in addition to the base classes.

articles.title: Hydia (Constellation)
articles.content:
Hydia is a prominent set of stars visible during summer in the northern hemisphere of the planet Skagmore, in the Fomalhaut system. It is one of the most prominent constellations of the world, highly visible during the evenings of summer nights. The originator of the constellation is unknown, but developed early in the colonization of Skagmore, and is named for a witch that features in local folklore.

Alpha (α) Hydia, the brightest star in the constellation, is among the brightest in Skagmore's night sky. Through it's stars general location on the galactic plane, Hydia is the namesake of the Hydian Sector (via the original Human name for the region, the Hydian Reaches). Alpha (α), Beta (β), Gamma (γ), and Delta (δ) Hydia all are host to habitable worlds. Gamma (γ) Hydia was later renamed to Nova Sol, following Human resettlement to the system.

Features

articles.title: Faster-than-Light Propulsion
articles.content:
Faster-than-light (also known as FTL or Superluminal) travel refers to the various methods used to traverse immense distances in a reasonable amount of time. The development of FTL travel by a civilization is a major milestone, typically serving as the dividing line for a civilization entering the "interstellar era". Without faster-than-light propulsion, interstellar communication, trade, and travel would be impossible within the life spans of most species in the galaxy. Starships without FTL capabilities are usually refered to as slow ships, slower-than-light or sublight ships, or sleeper ships; the latter refering to the use of cyrogenic sleep systems that enable a crew of a slow ship to survive the long journey.

FTL Methods

Various methods of FTL travel have been developed across the galaxy by different species, with the main archetypes being the following:

  • Warp Drive: The most common method of FTL travel, which involves distorting spacetime around a vessel to enable it to move through real-space at superluminal speeds. Early warp drives often faced the issue of producing a gamma-ray burst in front of the vessel; potentially causing harm to anything in front of the ship, or in some cases, to the ship itself. Notable examples of warp drives are the Apleen's Ourantan Device and Humanity's Spool Drive.
  • Jump/Instantaneous Drive: The rarest form of FTL travel (due to the limitations of traditional physics) a jump drive transports a vessel through real space instantaneously. Precise use of jump drives require extensive sensor data of the target location, and as such these drives typically have a shorter jump range, limited to a ship's long range sensors. The most notable form jump drive was developed by Equestrians - the Teleportation Megaspell.
  • Wormhole Generator: Another rare form of FTL, wormhole generators do as their name implies. Wormhole generators are often lumped in with jump drives, due to the instantaneous travel inside a wormhole. However, a ship must travel a short distance in real space to enter and exit a wormhole, and thus are not true jump drives. A large enough wormhole can enable an entire fleet to move in formation, instantaneously. However, wormhole generators are large, require extreme amounts of power, and are infamously unreliable with the exact endpoint of a wormhole, which has led to their relatively uncommon usage as FTL propulsion. Wormhole generators were often used by the military of United Equus and its successor state, the Solar Empire.
  • Slipspace Drive: Also known as slipdrives, this method is the fastest (aside from jump drives) and most efficient form of FTL travel currently known to exist. Slipspace drives operate by pushing a vessel into a higher dimension known as slipspace, where it can then move at extreme speeds without signifigant power expenditure. However, travel in slipspace is limited by natural filaments called slipspace routes, as vessels are only able to move via these routes. While it is theorized that every location in the galaxy is connected to a route, the varying intsensity of the filaments limit the realistic reach of slipspace. Even the most basic slipspace drives are able to access the strongest intensity routes, while less intense routes can only be accessed by progressively more complex and energy-demanding drives. As such, slipdrives are typically relegated as the secondary drive for vessels, useful only for long distance travel, while the first and final legs of a journey are completed via traditional FTL methods.

articles.title: Ferax
articles.content:
Ferax is a small moon orbiting the gas giant world of Ketoikana, in the Ikattomeno system. Once highly geologically active, rivaling the moon of Io in the Sol system, Ferax is now completely geologically dead. However, the remains of it's past volcanism are it's most notable feature: much of Ferax is now hollow - vast webs of lava tubes and empty magma chambers permeate throughout the entire moon.

Although the Ikattomeno system sits at a location of strategic importance, the parent star is among the most dangerous in the Hydian Sector, baking the entire system in extreme levels of solar radiation. However, thanks to Ferax's subterranean geography, the interior of the moon is protected from the effects of it's parent star, while still remaining accessible to the outside galaxy. Much of the interior is filled with an interconnected network of industrial zones and habitation areas, with Ferax now serving as the primary world of it's system.

The first species to discover and colonize Ferax were the Ichizhou, it would soon become a key piece of the Ichizhou Empire's border security. Essentially serving as the southwestern "gateway" to the empire, the moon's location was of particular interest to the Lethakos Corporation, the largest of the empire's mega-corporations, specializing in fuel and ship construction. Lethakos found Ferax useful for serving as a hub for it's various mining and exploratory interests, funding much of the construction of the moon's infrastructure. During the collapse of the Ichizhou Empire, Lethakos essentially served as the government of the moon, free to run it as they saw fit, far away from any oversight.

articles.title: Cloudsdale-class Colony Ship
articles.content:
The first Equestrian ship longer than 200 meters, the Cloudsdale-class (later referred to as the Cloudsdale I-class) colony ship was designed to act as a great ark for creatures from Equus looking to start a colony amongst the stars. The interior of it's iconic dome is filled terrain transplanted from Equus, with enough land to host a small village inside the ship. Standard equipment for the ship included two cloud generators, one on each side of the ship, intended to be used by Pegasi to create a cloud city centered around the ship. In addition to the amenities offered, the ship was also designed to carry up to 1,000 colonists, in order to deliver them to a proper ground based colony.

Production of the Cloudsdale-Class was immediately halted following the outbreak of the Great War, and the five ships of this class being constructed at the time were retooled into the combat oriented Cloudsdale II-class cruiser. Production would be resumed following the Battle of Caren Prime and the demilitarization of the Equestrian economy, but the class would quickly be rendered obsolete by the larger and more advanced Las Pegasus line of colony ships.

articles.title: Cloudsdale II-class Cruiser
articles.content:
Spurred by the outbreak of the Great War, the Cloudsdale II-class was an invention of necessity. It was a refit and overhaul of the existing Cloudsdale-class colony ship, which enabled rapid conversion of existing spaceframes. The initial design was drafted in less than two weeks by Commander (later Admiral) Nebula Bolt during the retreat from Yildun Rest, following the destruction of the Equestrian colony there.

The Cloudsdale II was the first real warship class of the Equestrian navy, with the civilian infrastructure present on the previous colony ships stripped out and exchanged for additional hull plating and weapon systems. The first five ships of this class were directly refitted from the five Cloudsdale-class colony ships currently being constructed at the time of the designing of the Cloudsdale II-class. The Cloudsdale II were surprisingly effective in their desired role, serving as the backbone of the Equestrian fleet until the introduction of the larger Hurricane-Class.

Even after the creation of more advanced cruisers, the Cloudsdale II continued to serve, mostly assigned to patrol duty to the fringes of Equestrian space. The last Cloudsdale IIs known to serve in combat saw action in Luna's Rebellion against the Solar Empire.

articles.title: Eclipse-Class Dreadnought
articles.content:
The Eclipse-class dreadnought was a heavy capital ship produced by [[United Equus]] during the Great War. Easily the single most formidable ship fielded by the [[Equus Star Navy]] in the war, only two were ever constructed due to the great costs involved. A notable break from typical Equestrian ship design tradition, the Eclipse-class was designed to be an instrument of both psychological and traditional warfare. The namesake ship of its class, the Eclipse was launched in 2436, and served under the direct command of Admiral Nebula Bolt, the designer of the Eclipse-class and many other of Equus' starships.

A second ship, the Penumbra (later rechristened as the Eclipse II), was built in secret as a part of the Pegas War Fleet.

The Eclipse-class was equipped with three superlaser cannons on it's underside, intended for use against enemy capital ships, like the A-1 supercruiser, but could easily be used against civilian targets, and carried enough firepower to level a city in a single blast, as first demonstrated during the Battle of Caren Prime. The Eclipse-class continued to be used even centuries after the Great War, a testament to it's effectiveness.

events.title: Discovery of the Elements of Harmony
Grogar's Defeat
events.description:
The earliest known ruler of the land that would eventually become Equestria, Grogar was a mysterious tyrant emperor and creator of all monsters that roam Equus.

Eventually, his tyranny was challenged by Gusty the Great and her unicorn warriors, who are theorized to have been an expedition from the north of Equestria. Gusty the Great was able to defeat Grogar after separating him from the source of his power: the Bewitching Bell.

Grogar was thus banished to the Realm of Shadows, and his bell hidden by Gusty in the hopes that nobody could use it's evil power again

events.title: Return of Nightmare Moon
events.description: Nightmare Moon – an evil being created by Princess Luna hosting the metamagical Tantabus parasite – returns from her 1000 year long banishment on Equus’ moon. Nightmare Moon and the Tantabus were neutralized by Twilight Sparkle and her friends using the rediscovered Elements of Harmony.

events.title: Canterlot Invasion of 1002
events.description: Queen Chrysalis kidnaps and disguises herself as Princess Cadence on the eve of Cadence's wedding in an attempt to conquer Canterlot and Equestria in a single decisive strike. Once revealed, her army of changelings wreaks havoc on the city before ultimately being defeated and expelled by the Cadence, her fiancé - Shining Armor, and the bearers of the Elements of Harmony.

The Great War

events.title: Formation of the Sector Alliance
events.description: With the outbreak of war between United Equus, the Sarden Clans, and the United Systems Confederace against the Apleen and Huachavi alliance, the Equestrians, Sarden, and Humans formalize their alliance. Princess Twilight Sparkle of Equestria, Peng Wei, the speaker of the Confederate Congress, and the Greenspeaker of Mari would meet aboard the *USS Enterprise* in the a centrally located system at the time simply known as F471, which would later become known as Terminus. Through this meeting of delegates the Sector Alliance was born, a common front against the Apleen and Huachavi.

events.title: Equus declares war on the Apleen and Huachavi
events.description: Following the devastating attack on Yildun Rest, the Convocation of Creatures on Equus voted unanimously to enter a state of war with the Apleen and Huachavi government, and approved the emergency reorganization of the Convocation into a legally-binding, planetwide government: United Equus.

events.title: Creation of Equus High Command
events.description: With the return of the survivors of the Battle of Yildun Rest back to Equus and the reorganization of the Conclave of Creatures into United Equus, a proper military command structure is formed. Prism Gleam and Nebula Bolt, both fresh veterans of Yildun Rest, senior officers on the ESAV Starswirl's maiden voyage, and long-time members of the Equestrian military, were made into the first two ranking Admirals. Prism Gleam, having served as the head of security in the Yildun Rest colony, was appointed as the commander-in-chief of the Equus Military Forces; while Nebula Bolt was assigned to overseeing the rapid build-up of Equus' military.=

events.title: Launch of the Cloudsdale-A
events.description: The first Cloudsdale II-class Cruiser, a design created from the earlier Cloudsdale-class colony ships in response to the outbreak of the great war, is launched from Equus Drydock. The Cloudsdale-A, named in honor of the colony ship destroyed in the first battle of Yildun Rest, was the first of what would become the mainstay of the Equestrian Navy for the most of the great war.

events.title: The Hauxipec Accords
events.description: The Hauxipec Accords was a meeting between the Continuation Forces and the Huachavi-Apleen Alliance that marked the final end of the Great War. The Huachavi was forced to give up all claims in the Disuputed Zone, while the remnant of the Apleen Fleet was to surrender immediately to the Apleen Republic or face complete destruction. Following the accords, the Preservers and Sarden both successfully rejoined the Sector Alliance, and the Human and Equestrian members of the Continuation Forces surrendered to their respective governments.

events.title: Dawn of the Hydian Peace
events.description: With reconstruction from the Great War complete, the powers of the Hydian Sector entered a prolonged period of peace and prosperity known as the Hydian Peace.

events.title: Start of the Olympus Ring
events.description: Construction began on the Olympus Ring, a massive orbital ring around Equus that would serve as a shipyard, star port, and orbital habitat for the planet. It would later prove to be a money pit, frequently behind schedule and above budget.

events.title: Puerto Paraíso Scandal
events.description: The Puerto Paraíso Scandal was first uncovered on August 16th, 2653, and would lead to the impeachment and imprisonment of President Gucharo Picorroca in October, and the resignation of Picorroca's entire cabinet, his successor, and 57 Convocation members in November. In the scandal's aftermath, Princess Celestia was appointed interim president on December 7th, 2653.

events.title: Equus Presidential Election of 2658
events.description: After serving the remainder of President Picorroca's term, President Celestia ran for reelection, winning a landslide 68.9% of the vote, beginning her 17th term in office.

events.title: Assassination of Princess Cadence
events.description: On the way back from a Sector Alliance diplomatic conference on Caren Prime, Princess Cadence, serving as the chief diplomat for Equus, was ambushed in deep space, in the vicinity of the Maw of Tartarus. The official investigation would place blame on the Huachavi, but the real perpetrators were a group of Chrysalid Changeling terrorists.

events.title: Equestrian Secession from the Sector Alliance
events.description: In the wake of Princess Cadence's assassination, Equus attempted to bring the Sector Alliance into a war with the Huachavi, who they believed was responsible for the attack. The other members of the alliance refused, which prompted the exit of the Union of Equus from the Sector Alliance.

events.title: Manehattan Race Riots
events.description: With a precipitous rise in xenophobia amongst Equestrians, tensions in Equus' most diverse city, Manehattan rose dramatically, reaching a head in the late winter of 2670. Sparked from a court ruling a Huachavi innocent of a crime levied against him, Equestrians took to the streets in a frenzy, starting a brutal, weeks long riot that left thousands dead, mostly non-Equestrian aliens.

events.title: Emergency Powers Act
events.description: Following the outset of the Manehattan Race Riots, the Convocation declared a state of emergency, granting President Celestia unilateral emergency powers and suspending the planned 2670 presidential election until further notice. Even after the end of the Manehattan Riots, the state of emergency persisted, and would not be removed until the Convocation disbanded itself in 2671.

events.title: Formation of the Solar Empire
events.description: Following the Battle of Nekmer, President Celestia declares herself to be the "Eternal Empress of Equus and it's Colonies". The government of United Equus dissolves itself and hands power over to Celestia.

events.title: The Marauder War
events.description: The Marauder War was an invasion and subsequent space conflict fought between the nascent Solar Empire and multiple Sarden clans that had long been raiding into Equestrian space. Pirate strongholds, including the planet Janset III, were captured or destroyed early in the war, with the following decade largely being composed of low level irregular combat against individuals or small groups of marauders.

events.title: Battle of Nekmer
events.description: On the 242nd anniversary of the 1st Battle of Yildun Rest and the start of the Great War, Equestrian naval forces engaged a hostile force above the colony of Nekmer, and were able to win the day. Equestrian officials stated that it was a group of Huachavi warships, though most analysts believe the Battle of Nekmer was a false flag attack orchestrated by President Celestia and the Equestrian Navy.

events.title: Imperial Tribunals on Drakan
events.description: WIP - Celestia uses the losses during the Drak'vorr occupation as reasoning to have the least loyal members of the military command investigated

events.title: Fall of the Solar Empire
events.description: Although at it's peak the Solar Empire controlled practically all of the Hydian Sector, in it's final years it began to face serious internal threats. However, forces such as Luna's Rebellion were never able to strike a killing blow to the Empire, such a thing would come during the cataclysmic Battle of Equus. In the days before the battle, the ESS Eclipse was rediscovered, and in it, the Tantabus. The Tantabus quickly spurred to action, activating what would be called the Nightmare Fleet, and attacked Equus. Through highly destructive means, the Tantabus forced the deposition of Empress Celestia, then disappeared just as quickly as he was found.

events.title: Foundation of the Union of Free Systems
